description: PayPal Pay Later promotional messaging SDK — displays financing banners on product, cart, and checkout pages

html_attribute_method.note: Messages render automatically when data-pp-message attribute is present on the element

javascript_method.code: paypal.Messages({ amount: 300, style: { layout: 'text', logo: { type: 'primary', position: 'left' }, text: { color: 'black' } } }).render('#message-container')

script_tags.option_b_js_dynamic: const sdkInstance = await window.paypal.createInstance({ clientId: 'YOUR_CLIENT_ID', components: ['paypal-messages'] });

amount.note: Number string with up to 2 decimal places

logo-type.constraints: MONOGRAM requires logo-position=LEFT; TEXT requires logo-position=INLINE

auto-bootstrap.note: If true, PayPal data layer auto-fetches content. If false, must use fetchContent().

setContent.description: Set fetched content on the message component

getFetchContentOptions.description: Extract config from HTML attributes for use in fetchContent
getFetchContentOptions.returns: Object with config from HTML attributes

fetchContent.description: Fetch message content from PayPal servers

options.onReady: callback when content is received (cache or server)
options.onContentReady: callback when content is received from server (takes precedence over onReady)
options.onTemplateReady: callback when content is received from cache (takes precedence over onReady)

options.onCalculate: callback when buyer enters amount in calculator
options.onShow: callback when Learn More is shown
options.onClose: callback when Learn More is closed

clientId.note: Recommended. Your PayPal client ID. Mutually exclusive with clientToken.

clientToken.note: Server-generated token. Mutually exclusive with clientId.

html.description: Web component attributes + minimal JS. Simplest approach.

javascript.description: Minimal HTML, JS fetchContent() drives config. Uses onReady callback with setContent().

hybrid.description: HTML attributes for static config + JS getFetchContentOptions() for dynamic. Combines both.

amount_update.v5: Re-render with new amount or use paypal.Messages().render() again

style_mapping.v5_layout_text: v6 uses web component attributes (logo-type, text-color, etc.)

product_page.what: Pay Later message with product price as amount
product_page.why: Awareness — buyer sees financing before adding to cart

cart_page.what: Pay Later message with cart total as amount
cart_page.why: Reinforcement — dynamic amount updates as cart changes

product_listing.what: Pay Later message per product tile

best_practices: Place messages on product, cart, and checkout pages for maximum impact
Always set amount attribute to show accurate financing info
Update amount dynamically when cart changes
Do NOT modify or create custom Pay Later messaging content
Test in sandbox to verify messages render correctly
